# Sprint 1: Runtime Core & Precision Timing Foundation

**Duration:** 2 weeks  
**Sprint Goal:** Establish the foundational runtime kernel with verifiable precision timing capabilities  
**Success Criteria:** Achieve ≤2ms stimulus onset timing on target hardware configuration

---

## Sprint Objectives

1. **High-Precision Clock Implementation**: Build microsecond-accurate timing foundation using Web Audio API
2. **Hierarchical Scheduler**: Implement the core scheduling system for experiment execution
3. **Timing Validation Framework**: Create comprehensive timing measurement and validation tools
4. **Core Data Structures**: Define fundamental experiment representation formats
5. **Performance Baseline**: Establish timing benchmarks across target platforms

---

## Sprint Backlog

### Epic: High-Precision Timing System
**Story Points: 8**

#### KER-001: High-Precision Clock Implementation
**Points: 5 | Priority: Critical**

**Description:** Implement the core timing system that will serve as the foundation for all experiment timing guarantees.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Integrate Web Audio API hardware clock for microsecond precision
- Implement RequestAnimationFrame synchronization for display timing
- Create Performance.now() wrapper for high-resolution timestamps
- Build timing drift detection and correction mechanisms

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Web Audio context provides stable, monotonic timestamps
- [ ] RAF callbacks synchronized with display refresh rate
- [ ] Performance.now() measurements accurate to 5μs resolution
- [ ] Timing drift detection warns when precision degrades
- [ ] Cross-browser compatibility validated (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)

**Technical Implementation:**
```typescript
// @psyforge/timing
export class PrecisionClock {
  private audioContext: AudioContext;
  private rafCallbacks: Set<(timestamp: number) => void>;
  private driftDetector: TimingDriftDetector;
  
  constructor() {
    this.audioContext = new AudioContext();
    this.rafCallbacks = new Set();
    this.driftDetector = new TimingDriftDetector();
  }
  
  // Get hardware-precision timestamp
  now(): number;
  
  // Schedule callback at specific time
  schedule(callback: () => void, targetTime: number): void;
  
  // Validate timing environment
  validateEnvironment(): TimingCapabilities;
}
```

---

#### KER-002: Hierarchical Scheduler Implementation  
**Points: 3 | Priority: High**

**Description:** Build the core experiment execution scheduler that manages timelines, trials, and stimulus presentation.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Implement priority-based task scheduling
- Support nested timeline execution with inheritance
- Handle concurrent task management
- Provide frame-accurate stimulus timing

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Schedule tasks with millisecond precision
- [ ] Support timeline hierarchy (experiment → timeline → trial → stimulus)
- [ ] Handle stimulus onset/offset timing accurately
- [ ] Manage concurrent executions without timing conflicts
- [ ] Provide debugging/profiling hooks for timing analysis

**Technical Implementation:**
```typescript
// @psyforge/scheduler
export class HierarchicalScheduler {
  private clock: PrecisionClock;
  private taskQueue: PriorityQueue<ScheduledTask>;
  private activeTimelines: Map<string, Timeline>;
  
  // Schedule experiment execution
  scheduleExperiment(experiment: Experiment): Promise<void>;
  
  // Execute timeline with precise timing
  executeTimeline(timeline: Timeline): Promise<void>;
  
  // Handle stimulus presentation
  presentStimulus(stimulus: Stimulus, timing: TimingSpec): Promise<void>;
}
```

---

### Epic: Timing Validation & Benchmarking
**Story Points: 8**

#### TST-001: Timing Benchmark Harness v1
**Points: 5 | Priority: High**

**Description:** Create comprehensive timing validation tools to verify and document precision capabilities.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Build automated timing benchmark suite
- Implement photodiode/oscilloscope integration for external validation
- Create cross-platform timing capability assessment
- Generate precision validation reports

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Automated benchmark measures stimulus onset accuracy
- [ ] External hardware validation (photodiode) confirms timing
- [ ] Cross-browser/OS timing capability matrix generated
- [ ] Performance regression detection implemented
- [ ] Timing validation reports exportable for publication

**Hardware Requirements:**
- High-speed camera (240fps+) for visual validation
- Photodiode + oscilloscope for electrical validation  
- Multiple test systems (Windows/Mac/Linux + different browsers)

---

#### KER-005: Cross-OS Timer Drift Detection
**Points: 3 | Priority: Medium**

**Description:** Implement detection and mitigation for OS-level timer throttling and drift.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Detect browser tab visibility throttling
- Identify OS power management interference
- Warn users when timing environment is compromised
- Provide automatic environment optimization suggestions

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Detect visibility-based timer throttling
- [ ] Identify OS power management impacts
- [ ] Display real-time timing health indicators
- [ ] Provide actionable optimization recommendations
- [ ] Log timing degradation events for analysis

---

### Epic: Core Data Structures & API Design
**Story Points: 7**

#### DSL-001: Define Initial AST Schema (Zod)
**Points: 4 | Priority: Critical**

**Description:** Define the canonical Abstract Syntax Tree schema that represents experiments.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Create Zod schemas for all experiment components
- Define TypeScript types from schemas
- Implement runtime validation
- Support schema versioning and migration

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Complete Zod schema for Experiment, Timeline, Trial, Stimulus
- [ ] TypeScript types auto-generated from schemas
- [ ] Runtime validation prevents invalid experiment structures
- [ ] Schema versioning supports backward compatibility
- [ ] JSON Schema generation for interoperability

**Schema Structure:**
```typescript
// Core experiment components
const ExperimentSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  name: z.string(),
  version: z.string(),
  timelines: z.array(TimelineSchema),
  metadata: z.record(z.any()).optional()
});

const TimelineSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  trials: z.array(TrialSchema),
  randomization: RandomizationSchema.optional(),
  repetitions: z.number().default(1)
});

const TrialSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  stimuli: z.array(StimulusSchema),
  responses: z.array(ResponseSchema),
  timing: TimingSchema
});
```

---

#### DSL-002: Implement FZB Serialization
**Points: 3 | Priority: High**

**Description:** Create the Forge Binary AST (FZB) serialization format for canonical experiment representation.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Protocol Buffers schema for binary serialization
- Version-pinned experiment representation
- Efficient serialization/deserialization
- Cross-platform compatibility

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Protocol Buffers schema matches Zod schema structure
- [ ] Binary serialization reduces experiment size by >70%
- [ ] Deserialization preserves exact experiment semantics
- [ ] Version information embedded in binary format
- [ ] Cross-language compatibility (TypeScript, Python, R)

---

### Epic: Infrastructure & Development Setup
**Story Points: 5**

#### INF-001: Stand up Monorepo & CI/CD
**Points: 3 | Priority: Critical**

**Description:** Establish the development infrastructure for the multi-package Psycho-Forge ecosystem.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Nx monorepo with TypeScript packages
- Automated testing and linting in CI
- Package publishing to private npm registry
- Code quality gates and PR validation

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Nx workspace configured with core packages
- [ ] GitHub Actions CI runs tests on all PRs
- [ ] ESLint + Prettier enforced across codebase
- [ ] Private npm registry configured for package distribution
- [ ] PR merge requires passing tests + code review

**Package Structure:**
```
packages/
├── core/           # Core experiment types and utilities
├── timing/         # High-precision timing implementation
├── scheduler/      # Hierarchical task scheduler
├── runtime/        # Experiment execution engine
├── serialization/  # FZB binary format handling
└── validation/     # Schema validation and testing
```

---

#### ARC-001: Spike: Kernel-to-Renderer PoC
**Points: 2 | Priority: Medium**

**Description:** Proof-of-concept for integrating timing kernel with PixiJS rendering pipeline.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Basic PixiJS application with precise timing
- Demonstrate stimulus presentation with measured accuracy
- Validate WebGL2 performance characteristics
- Establish rendering pipeline architecture

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] PixiJS renders stimuli with frame-accurate timing
- [ ] WebGL2 pipeline bypasses DOM for critical path
- [ ] Stimulus presentation measured under 2ms variability
- [ ] Rendering architecture supports complex stimulus types
- [ ] Performance profiling shows rendering bottlenecks

---

## Definition of Done

**For each ticket:**
- [ ] Code implemented and unit tested (>90% coverage)
- [ ] Integration tests validate cross-component functionality
- [ ] Performance benchmarks meet sprint targets
- [ ] Documentation updated (API docs + implementation notes)
- [ ] Code review completed by another team member
- [ ] **Gemini Pro code audit approval received**
- [ ] **O3 (via zen mcp) code audit approval received**

**For sprint completion:**
- [ ] All tickets meet DoD criteria
- [ ] ≤2ms timing demonstrated on target hardware
- [ ] Timing validation report generated and reviewed
- [ ] Technical debt items documented for future sprints
- [ ] Sprint retrospective completed with lessons learned

---

## Quality Gates & Audit Requirements

### Pre-Sprint Advancement Checklist

**Code Quality Audit (Gemini Pro):**
- [ ] Architecture review confirms scalable design patterns
- [ ] Code quality meets enterprise standards
- [ ] Performance characteristics validated
- [ ] Security considerations addressed
- [ ] Documentation completeness verified

**Technical Implementation Audit (O3 via zen mcp):**
- [ ] Algorithm correctness validated
- [ ] Edge case handling comprehensive
- [ ] Error conditions properly managed
- [ ] Integration points well-defined
- [ ] Testing strategy covers critical paths

**Acceptance Testing:**
- [ ] All unit tests pass (>90% coverage)
- [ ] Integration tests validate end-to-end workflows
- [ ] Performance benchmarks meet timing requirements
- [ ] Cross-platform validation completed
- [ ] Documentation reviewed and approved

---

## Risk Mitigation

**Technical Risks:**
- **Web Audio API limitations**: Fallback to Performance.now() with degraded precision warnings
- **Cross-browser timing variations**: Comprehensive testing matrix with environment-specific optimizations
- **Performance bottlenecks**: Early profiling and benchmark-driven optimization

**Delivery Risks:**
- **Complex timing requirements**: Focus on single-platform validation first, expand gradually
- **Integration complexity**: Modular architecture allows independent development and testing

---

## Sprint Success Metrics

**Primary Metrics:**
- **Timing Precision**: ≤2ms stimulus onset jitter on target hardware
- **Performance**: Sustain 144Hz refresh rate with complex stimuli
- **Reliability**: Zero timing failures in 1000-trial test sequences

**Secondary Metrics:**
- **Code Quality**: 90%+ test coverage, zero critical issues in audits
- **Documentation**: Complete API documentation for all public interfaces
- **Team Velocity**: Sprint completed within 2-week timeframe

---

*Next: [Sprint 2: Compiler & First Test](sprint2.md)*

**Integration Scope:**
- [ ] DataCite API integration for DOI assignment
- [ ] Zenodo repository connection
- [ ] Automatic citation generation
- [ ] Reproducibility badge system

**Dependencies**: Experiment repository (Sprint 7)  
**Risk Level**: Low  
**Notes**: Key component of network effects strategy

---

## Blocked Items

### Currently No Blocked Items
All identified tasks can proceed with appropriate resource allocation.

### Potential Future Blockers

#### Academic Validation Requirements
- **Blocker**: Need IRB approval for validation studies
- **Impact**: Could delay academic publication timeline
- **Mitigation**: Begin IRB process early, have backup validation approaches

#### Hardware Testing Complexity  
- **Blocker**: Specialized timing measurement equipment availability
- **Impact**: Could delay timing precision validation
- **Mitigation**: Partner with existing psychophysics labs for testing

#### Browser API Limitations
- **Blocker**: Web Audio API restrictions or changes
- **Impact**: Could compromise timing precision claims
- **Mitigation**: Maintain fallback timing strategies, desktop app alternative

---

## Resource Requirements

### Development Team (Immediate Need)
- **Senior Frontend Developer** (TypeScript, SvelteKit, WebGL)
- **Senior Backend Developer** (Node.js, databases, API design)  
- **UI/UX Designer** (accessibility, scientific workflows)
- **DevOps Engineer** (CI/CD, monitoring, deployment)
- **Academic Relations Specialist** (research community connections)

### Equipment & Infrastructure
- **Development Hardware**: High-performance workstations for each developer
- **Testing Equipment**: Timing validation hardware suite ($10-15k estimated)
- **Cloud Infrastructure**: CI/CD, monitoring, private npm registry
- **Software Licenses**: Development tools, monitoring services

### Budget Estimates
- **Team (6 months)**: $400-500k (depending on location/seniority)
- **Equipment**: $25-30k (hardware, testing, infrastructure)  
- **Services**: $5-10k (cloud, monitoring, tools)
- **Total Sprint 1-5**: $430-540k estimated

---

## Success Dependencies

### Technical Milestones
1. **Sprint 1**: ≤2ms timing precision demonstrated
2. **Sprint 2**: Complete experiment compiles and executes
3. **Sprint 3**: GUI provides intuitive experiment building
4. **Sprint 5**: Real-time collaboration functional
5. **Sprint 10**: 3 founding labs running production studies

### Strategic Milestones  
1. **Academic Paper**: Timing validation study submitted to peer review
2. **Founding Labs**: 5 research partnerships established
3. **DOI Integration**: Automatic citation system functional
4. **Network Effects**: First viral experiment sharing demonstrated
5. **Market Validation**: Clear demand from research community

---

## Risk Monitoring

### High-Risk Items Requiring Attention
- **Timing Precision Validation**: Single point of failure for entire value proposition
- **Academic Community Adoption**: Without researcher buy-in, technical excellence is meaningless
- **Team Formation**: Need senior developers with specific expertise
- **Browser API Stability**: Dependency on evolving web standards

### Early Warning Indicators
- Timing validation tests failing on any target platform
- Academic advisor feedback indicating skepticism
- Difficulty recruiting qualified development team
- Web API deprecation or restriction announcements

---

*Next tasks updated weekly. Priority items reviewed daily during active development.*

**Current Focus**: Complete sprint designs to enable full project planning and team formation.

# Sprint 5: Simulation & Intelligence

**Duration:** 2 weeks  
**Sprint Goal:** Implement AI-assisted design and headless simulation capabilities for testing and validation  
**Success Criteria:** AI command palette functional and time-travel simulator provides accurate experiment preview

---

## Sprint Objectives

1. **Headless Runtime**: Package ForgeScript runtime for WebAssembly execution and simulation
2. **AI Command Palette**: Natural language experiment design assistance  
3. **Time-Travel Simulator**: Interactive experiment preview with scrubbing timeline
4. **Virtual Device Lab**: Simulation environment for testing without hardware
5. **Performance Optimization**: Memory budget enforcement and optimization

---

## Sprint Backlog

### Epic: Headless Simulation Engine
**Story Points: 9**

#### SIM-001: Package @psyforge/headless (WASM) & Memory Budget Test
**Points: 6 | Priority: Critical**

**Description:** Create a WebAssembly-compiled version of the ForgeScript runtime for high-performance simulation and testing.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Compile ForgeScript runtime to WebAssembly for deterministic execution
- Implement virtual time progression for accelerated simulation
- Memory management with strict budget enforcement (<100MB target)
- Statistical models for generating realistic participant responses

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] WASM runtime executes ForgeScript experiments deterministically
- [ ] Virtual time allows simulation at 100x+ real-time speed
- [ ] Memory usage stays under 100MB for standard experiments (200+ trials)
- [ ] Response generation uses configurable statistical models
- [ ] Headless execution produces identical results across runs

**WASM Implementation:**
```typescript
// @psyforge/headless
export interface HeadlessConfig {
  experiment: CompiledExperiment;
  cognitiveModel: CognitiveModel;
  participants: number;
  speedMultiplier: number; // 1 = real-time, 100 = 100x speed
  randomSeed?: number;     // For reproducible simulation

    30→- Fastify-based microservice with high performance routing

    47→import Fastify from 'fastify';
    48→import jwt from '@fastify/jwt';
    49→import { DatabaseManager } from './database';

**Success Criteria:** Automated participant recruitment works with Prolific integration and OAuth2 authentication

---

## Sprint Objectives

1. **ForgeRouter Service**: Build the participant session management and assignment system
2. **Universal Recruitment Fabric**: Implement automated recruitment with provider-agnostic API
3. **OAuth2 Authentication**: Secure authentication flow for researchers and participants
4. **Global State Management**: Transactional counter assignment and session persistence
5. **Security Foundation**: Implement comprehensive security measures and audit compliance

---

## Sprint Backlog

### Epic: ForgeRouter Service Architecture
**Story Points: 8**

#### SRV-001: Build ForgeRouter Service
**Points: 5 | Priority: Critical**

**Description:** Create the core service that handles participant routing, session assignment, and experiment launching.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Fastify-based microservice with high performance routing
- JWT-based session token generation and validation
- Participant ID management and session tracking
- Integration with recruitment providers
- Comprehensive logging and monitoring

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Service handles participant URLs from recruitment platforms
- [ ] Session assignment atomic and collision-free
- [ ] JWT tokens generated with appropriate claims and expiration
- [ ] Participant routing works with query parameters from different providers
- [ ] Service scales to handle 1000+ concurrent participant assignments
- [ ] Comprehensive API documentation and monitoring

# Psycho-Forge Development Team Job Descriptions

**Project:** Psycho-Forge - The Scientific Operating System for Behavioral Research  
**Company:** Psycho-Forge Labs  
**Location:** Remote-first with optional co-working  
**Employment Type:** Full-time, Contract, or Consultant  
**Updated:** July 2025

---

## Mission Statement

We are building the platform that will **end the reproducibility crisis in behavioral research**. Every role demands precision engineering, scientific rigor, and commitment to quality that will define the next era of psychological science.

### Universal Requirements (All Roles)

- **Precision Mindset**: Commitment to ≤2ms timing accuracy and zero-compromise quality
- **Scientific Understanding**: Appreciation for research methodology and scientific integrity
- **Open Source Experience**: Demonstrated contributions to public repositories
- **Quality Gates**: Willingness to work within mandatory AI audit processes (Gemini Pro + O3)
- **Collaborative Spirit**: Experience with remote teams and asynchronous communication
- **Performance Focus**: High-performance web applications and desktop-class experiences

---

## 1. Senior Frontend Developer - Timing Precision Specialist

### Role Overview
Lead the development of the world's most precise web-based experiment platform, achieving sub-2ms timing accuracy that rivals desktop applications.

### Key Responsibilities
- **High-Precision Timing**: Implement Web Audio API clock synchronization and RequestAnimationFrame optimization
- **WebGL/PixiJS Graphics**: Build stimulus rendering engine with frame-perfect accuracy
- **TypeScript Architecture**: Design type-safe, maintainable frontend architecture
- **SvelteKit Development**: Create responsive, accessible user interfaces
- **Performance Optimization**: Achieve 144Hz frame rates with minimal jitter
- **Cross-Browser Testing**: Ensure timing precision across all major browsers
- **Scientific Integration**: Collaborate with researchers to understand timing requirements

### Technical Requirements
- **TypeScript Expert**: 5+ years with complex type systems and generics
- **WebGL/Graphics**: Experience with PixiJS, Three.js, or similar rendering engines
- **Timing Precision**: Knowledge of browser performance APIs and timing optimization
- **SvelteKit Proficiency**: 2+ years with Svelte ecosystem
- **Web Audio API**: Experience with audio timing and synchronization
- **Performance Profiling**: Skills with Chrome DevTools and performance analysis
- **Testing**: Unit testing, integration testing, and timing validation

### Scientific Context
- Understanding of psychophysics and reaction time research

# Next Tasks Queue - Psycho-Forge Development

**Project:** Psycho-Forge - The Scientific Operating System for Behavioral Research  
**Current Phase:** Pre-Development Planning  
**Last Updated:** July 2025

---

## Immediate Priority (Next Phase)

### 🏗️ 1. High-Precision Clock Implementation (KER-001)
**Status**: Ready to Start  
**Assigned to**: Developer  
**Due**: Sprint 1 completion  
**Priority**: Critical  
**Story Points**: 5

**Description:** Implement the core timing system that will serve as the foundation for all experiment timing guarantees.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Integrate Web Audio API hardware clock for microsecond precision
- [ ] Implement RequestAnimationFrame synchronization for display timing
- [ ] Create Performance.now() wrapper for high-resolution timestamps
- [ ] Build timing drift detection and correction mechanisms

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Web Audio context provides stable, monotonic timestamps
- [ ] RAF callbacks synchronized with display refresh rate
- [ ] Performance.now() measurements accurate to 5μs resolution
- [ ] Timing drift detection warns when precision degrades
- [ ] Cross-browser compatibility validated (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)

**Package Structure:**
```
packages/timing/
├── src/
│   ├── PrecisionClock.ts
│   ├── TimingDriftDetector.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── tests/
└── package.json
```

**Dependencies**: Nx monorepo setup, testing infrastructure  
**Risk Level**: High (foundational for entire platform)  
**Notes**: This is the critical first implementation for ≤2ms timing precision

### ✅ 2. Create Development Team Job Descriptions
**Status**: Completed  
**Assigned to**: Project Lead  
**Completed**: July 8, 2025  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Job Description Requirements:**
- [x] **Senior Frontend Developer** - TypeScript, SvelteKit, WebGL/PixiJS, timing precision expertise
- [x] **Senior Backend Developer** - Node.js, PostgreSQL, API design, scientific data handling
- [x] **UI/UX Designer** - Accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA), scientific workflows, Figma
- [x] **DevOps Engineer** - Kubernetes, CI/CD, monitoring, performance optimization
- [x] **Academic Relations Lead** - Psychology background, research connections, publication experience

**Key Requirements for All Roles:**
- Strong technical skills with demonstrated open-source contributions
- Experience with high-performance web applications
- Understanding of scientific software requirements
- Commitment to precision engineering and quality standards
- Ability to work within mandatory audit gates (Gemini Pro + O3)

**Deliverable**: Complete job descriptions created in `job-descriptions.md`  
**Risk Level**: High (team quality determines project success)  
**Notes**: Job descriptions emphasize precision timing requirements and scientific rigor

### 2. Form Core Development Team  
**Status**: Ready to Start (job descriptions completed)
**Assigned to**: Project Lead  
**Due**: Within 2 weeks  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Hiring Requirements:**
- [ ] Post job descriptions to technical recruitment platforms
- [ ] Conduct initial candidate screening and technical assessments
- [ ] Complete team interviews and cultural fit evaluation
- [ ] Extend offers to selected candidates
- [ ] Plan 30-day onboarding program for new team members

**Dependencies**: Budget approval, job descriptions (✅ completed)
**Risk Level**: High  
**Notes**: Begin hiring process immediately with completed job descriptions

### ✅ 3. Create Recruitment Strategy and Platform Postings
**Status**: Completed  
**Assigned to**: Project Lead  
**Completed**: July 8, 2025  
**Priority**: High  

**Recruitment Strategy Requirements:**
- [x] Identify target recruitment platforms (AngelList, Stack Overflow, GitHub Jobs, academic networks)
- [x] Create compelling job postings adapted for each platform
- [x] Develop technical assessment criteria and coding challenges
- [x] Plan interview process and evaluation rubrics
- [x] Establish compensation benchmarks and equity guidelines
- [x] Create talent sourcing strategy (LinkedIn, GitHub, academic conferences)

**Key Platforms for Posting:**
- **Technical Roles**: Stack Overflow Jobs, GitHub Jobs, AngelList, RemoteOK
- **Academic Role**: Chronicle of Higher Education, Inside Higher Ed, PsyJobSearch
- **General**: LinkedIn, Indeed, company website careers page
- **Specialized**: Y Combinator Work List, RemoteOK, FlexJobs

**Deliverable**: Complete recruitment strategy created in `recruitment-strategy.md`  
**Dependencies**: Job descriptions (✅ completed), budget approval
**Risk Level**: Medium  
**Notes**: Comprehensive strategy with platform-specific postings, technical assessments, and 8-week timeline

### 4. Execute Recruitment Campaign Launch
**Status**: Ready to Start  
**Assigned to**: Project Lead  
**Due**: Within 1 week  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Campaign Launch Requirements:**
- [ ] Set up accounts on all target platforms (Stack Overflow, GitHub Jobs, AngelList, etc.)
- [ ] Publish platform-specific job postings with tracking links
- [ ] Configure LinkedIn Recruiter and GitHub Talent sourcing tools
- [ ] Launch active sourcing campaigns with personalized outreach
- [ ] Implement candidate tracking and metrics dashboard
- [ ] Begin 50+ weekly outreach per role via LinkedIn and GitHub

**Key Deliverables:**
- Live job postings on 8 target platforms

- Candidate management system with automated workflows
- Weekly metrics reporting on application volume and quality
- Technical assessment system ready for qualified candidates

**Dependencies**: Recruitment strategy (✅ completed), budget approval, platform access
**Risk Level**: High (delays team formation)  
**Notes**: Implementation of comprehensive recruitment strategy with immediate execution focus

---

## Completed Tasks

## Next Sprint Development (When Team Forms)

- **Academic Trust is Paramount**: Peer-reviewed publication must come before marketing or adoption efforts

- **Competitive Advantage**: Target architectural weaknesses, not feature comparison

# Next Tasks Queue - Psycho-Forge Development

**Project:** Psycho-Forge - The Scientific Operating System for Behavioral Research  
**Current Phase:** Pre-Development Planning  
**Last Updated:** July 2025

---

## Immediate Priority (Next Phase)

### 🏗️ 1. High-Precision Clock Implementation (KER-001)
**Status**: Ready to Start  
**Assigned to**: Developer  
**Due**: Sprint 1 completion  
**Priority**: Critical  
**Story Points**: 5

**Description:** Implement the core timing system that will serve as the foundation for all experiment timing guarantees.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Integrate Web Audio API hardware clock for microsecond precision
- [ ] Implement RequestAnimationFrame synchronization for display timing
- [ ] Create Performance.now() wrapper for high-resolution timestamps
- [ ] Build timing drift detection and correction mechanisms

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Web Audio context provides stable, monotonic timestamps
- [ ] RAF callbacks synchronized with display refresh rate
- [ ] Performance.now() measurements accurate to 5μs resolution
- [ ] Timing drift detection warns when precision degrades
- [ ] Cross-browser compatibility validated (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)

**Package Structure:**
```
packages/timing/
├── src/
│   ├── PrecisionClock.ts
│   ├── TimingDriftDetector.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── tests/
└── package.json
```

**Dependencies**: Nx monorepo setup, testing infrastructure  
**Risk Level**: High (foundational for entire platform)  
**Notes**: This is the critical first implementation for ≤2ms timing precision

### ✅ 2. Create Development Team Job Descriptions
**Status**: Completed  
**Assigned to**: Project Lead  
**Completed**: July 8, 2025  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Job Description Requirements:**
- [x] **Senior Frontend Developer** - TypeScript, SvelteKit, WebGL/PixiJS, timing precision expertise
- [x] **Senior Backend Developer** - Node.js, PostgreSQL, API design, scientific data handling
- [x] **UI/UX Designer** - Accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA), scientific workflows, Figma
- [x] **DevOps Engineer** - Kubernetes, CI/CD, monitoring, performance optimization
- [x] **Academic Relations Lead** - Psychology background, research connections, publication experience

**Key Requirements for All Roles:**
- Strong technical skills with demonstrated open-source contributions
- Experience with high-performance web applications
- Understanding of scientific software requirements
- Commitment to precision engineering and quality standards
- Ability to work within mandatory audit gates (Gemini Pro + O3)

**Deliverable**: Complete job descriptions created in `job-descriptions.md`  
**Risk Level**: High (team quality determines project success)  
**Notes**: Job descriptions emphasize precision timing requirements and scientific rigor

### 2. Form Core Development Team  
**Status**: Ready to Start (job descriptions completed)
**Assigned to**: Project Lead  
**Due**: Within 2 weeks  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Hiring Requirements:**
- [ ] Post job descriptions to technical recruitment platforms
- [ ] Conduct initial candidate screening and technical assessments
- [ ] Complete team interviews and cultural fit evaluation
- [ ] Extend offers to selected candidates
- [ ] Plan 30-day onboarding program for new team members

**Dependencies**: Budget approval, job descriptions (✅ completed)
**Risk Level**: High  
**Notes**: Begin hiring process immediately with completed job descriptions

### ✅ 3. Create Recruitment Strategy and Platform Postings
**Status**: Completed  
**Assigned to**: Project Lead  
**Completed**: July 8, 2025  
**Priority**: High  

**Recruitment Strategy Requirements:**
- [x] Identify target recruitment platforms (AngelList, Stack Overflow, GitHub Jobs, academic networks)
- [x] Create compelling job postings adapted for each platform
- [x] Develop technical assessment criteria and coding challenges
- [x] Plan interview process and evaluation rubrics
- [x] Establish compensation benchmarks and equity guidelines
- [x] Create talent sourcing strategy (LinkedIn, GitHub, academic conferences)

**Key Platforms for Posting:**
- **Technical Roles**: Stack Overflow Jobs, GitHub Jobs, AngelList, RemoteOK
- **Academic Role**: Chronicle of Higher Education, Inside Higher Ed, PsyJobSearch
- **General**: LinkedIn, Indeed, company website careers page
- **Specialized**: Y Combinator Work List, RemoteOK, FlexJobs

**Deliverable**: Complete recruitment strategy created in `recruitment-strategy.md`  
**Dependencies**: Job descriptions (✅ completed), budget approval
**Risk Level**: Medium  
**Notes**: Comprehensive strategy with platform-specific postings, technical assessments, and 8-week timeline

### 4. Execute Recruitment Campaign Launch
**Status**: Ready to Start  
**Assigned to**: Project Lead  
**Due**: Within 1 week  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Campaign Launch Requirements:**
- [ ] Set up accounts on all target platforms (Stack Overflow, GitHub Jobs, AngelList, etc.)
- [ ] Publish platform-specific job postings with tracking links
- [ ] Configure LinkedIn Recruiter and GitHub Talent sourcing tools
- [ ] Launch active sourcing campaigns with personalized outreach
- [ ] Implement candidate tracking and metrics dashboard
- [ ] Begin 50+ weekly outreach per role via LinkedIn and GitHub

**Key Deliverables:**
- Live job postings on 8 target platforms
- Active sourcing campaigns generating 50+ applications per role
- Candidate management system with automated workflows
- Weekly metrics reporting on application volume and quality
- Technical assessment system ready for qualified candidates

**Dependencies**: Recruitment strategy (✅ completed), budget approval, platform access
**Risk Level**: High (delays team formation)  
**Notes**: Implementation of comprehensive recruitment strategy with immediate execution focus

---

## Completed Tasks

- [x] **Sprint 9**: Polish & Alpha Prep (performance optimization, documentation)
- [x] **Sprint 10**: Alpha Release (deployment, founding lab onboarding)

**Outcome**: Complete 10-sprint roadmap ready for development team

## Next Sprint Development (When Team Forms)

- RequestAnimationFrame display synchronization
- Performance.now() high-resolution timestamps
- Cross-browser timing validation

**Dependencies**: Development environment, hardware testing setup  
**Risk Level**: High  
**Notes**: This is the foundational technical achievement for the entire platform

### 5. Hierarchical Scheduler (KER-002)  
**Status**: Designed, Not Started  
**Assigned to**: TBD (Senior Frontend Developer)  
**Due**: Sprint 1 completion  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Technical Scope:**
- Priority-based task scheduling
- Timeline hierarchy execution
- Frame-accurate stimulus timing
- Concurrent task management

**Risk Level**: Medium  
**Notes**: Builds directly on timing foundation

---

## Future Sprint Preparations

### 6. Academic Partnership Development
**Status**: Not Started  
**Assigned to**: TBD (Academic Relations Lead)  
**Due**: Before Alpha Release  
**Priority**: High  

**Partnership Requirements:**
- [ ] Identify 5 founding research labs
- [ ] Establish collaboration agreements

- [ ] Plan peer-review publication strategy

**Dependencies**: Working prototype (Sprint 2+ completion)  
**Risk Level**: Medium  
**Notes**: Critical for academic legitimacy strategy

### 7. DOI Integration Planning
**Status**: Researched, Not Started  
**Assigned to**: TBD (Backend Developer)  
**Due**: Sprint 7 completion  
**Priority**: Medium  

**Integration Scope:**
- [ ] DataCite API integration for DOI assignment
- [ ] Zenodo repository connection
- [ ] Automatic citation generation
- [ ] Reproducibility badge system

**Dependencies**: Experiment repository (Sprint 7)  
**Risk Level**: Low  
**Notes**: Key component of network effects strategy

---

## Blocked Items

### Currently No Blocked Items
All identified tasks can proceed with appropriate resource allocation.

### Potential Future Blockers

#### Academic Validation Requirements
- **Blocker**: Need IRB approval for validation studies
- **Impact**: Could delay academic publication timeline
- **Mitigation**: Begin IRB process early, have backup validation approaches

#### Hardware Testing Complexity  
- **Blocker**: Specialized timing measurement equipment availability
- **Impact**: Could delay timing precision validation
- **Mitigation**: Partner with existing psychophysics labs for testing

#### Browser API Limitations
- **Blocker**: Web Audio API restrictions or changes
- **Impact**: Could compromise timing precision claims
- **Mitigation**: Maintain fallback timing strategies, desktop app alternative

---

## Resource Requirements

### Development Team (Immediate Need)
- **Senior Frontend Developer** (TypeScript, SvelteKit, WebGL)
- **Senior Backend Developer** (Node.js, databases, API design)  
- **UI/UX Designer** (accessibility, scientific workflows)
- **DevOps Engineer** (CI/CD, monitoring, deployment)
- **Academic Relations Specialist** (research community connections)

### Equipment & Infrastructure
- **Development Hardware**: High-performance workstations for each developer
- **Testing Equipment**: Timing validation hardware suite ($10-15k estimated)
- **Cloud Infrastructure**: CI/CD, monitoring, private npm registry
- **Software Licenses**: Development tools, monitoring services

### Budget Estimates
- **Team (6 months)**: $400-500k (depending on location/seniority)
- **Equipment**: $25-30k (hardware, testing, infrastructure)  
- **Services**: $5-10k (cloud, monitoring, tools)
- **Total Sprint 1-5**: $430-540k estimated

---

## Success Dependencies

### Technical Milestones
1. **Sprint 1**: ≤2ms timing precision demonstrated
2. **Sprint 2**: Complete experiment compiles and executes
3. **Sprint 3**: GUI provides intuitive experiment building
4. **Sprint 5**: Real-time collaboration functional
5. **Sprint 10**: 3 founding labs running production studies

### Strategic Milestones  
1. **Academic Paper**: Timing validation study submitted to peer review
2. **Founding Labs**: 5 research partnerships established
3. **DOI Integration**: Automatic citation system functional
4. **Network Effects**: First viral experiment sharing demonstrated
5. **Market Validation**: Clear demand from research community

---

## Risk Monitoring

### High-Risk Items Requiring Attention
- **Timing Precision Validation**: Single point of failure for entire value proposition
- **Academic Community Adoption**: Without researcher buy-in, technical excellence is meaningless
- **Team Formation**: Need senior developers with specific expertise
- **Browser API Stability**: Dependency on evolving web standards

### Early Warning Indicators
- Timing validation tests failing on any target platform
- Academic advisor feedback indicating skepticism
- Difficulty recruiting qualified development team
- Web API deprecation or restriction announcements

---

*Next tasks updated weekly. Priority items reviewed daily during active development.*

**Current Focus**: Complete sprint designs to enable full project planning and team formation.

- Priority-based task scheduling
- Timeline hierarchy execution
- Frame-accurate stimulus timing
- Concurrent task management

**Dependencies**: High-precision clock implementation  
**Risk Level**: Medium  
**Notes**: Builds directly on timing foundation

---

## Future Sprint Preparations


### 7. DOI Integration Planning
**Status**: Researched, Not Started  
**Assigned to**: TBD (Backend Developer)  
**Due**: Sprint 7 completion  
**Priority**: Medium  

**Integration Scope:**
- [ ] DataCite API integration for DOI assignment
- [ ] Zenodo repository connection
- [ ] Automatic citation generation
- [ ] Reproducibility badge system

**Dependencies**: Experiment repository (Sprint 7)  
**Risk Level**: Low  
**Notes**: Key component of network effects strategy

---

## Blocked Items

### Currently No Blocked Items
All identified tasks can proceed with appropriate resource allocation.

### Potential Future Blockers

#### Academic Validation Requirements
- **Blocker**: Need IRB approval for validation studies
- **Impact**: Could delay academic publication timeline
- **Mitigation**: Begin IRB process early, have backup validation approaches

#### Hardware Testing Complexity  
- **Blocker**: Specialized timing measurement equipment availability
- **Impact**: Could delay timing precision validation
- **Mitigation**: Partner with existing psychophysics labs for testing

#### Browser API Limitations
- **Blocker**: Web Audio API restrictions or changes
- **Impact**: Could compromise timing precision claims
- **Mitigation**: Maintain fallback timing strategies, desktop app alternative

---

## Resource Requirements

### Development Tools & Infrastructure
- **Code Quality**: ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript strict mode
- **Testing**: Vitest, Testing Library, Playwright for e2e
- **Monitoring**: Sentry for error tracking and performance
- **Documentation**: TypeDoc for API docs, Storybook for components

### Equipment & Infrastructure
- **Development Hardware**: High-performance workstations for each developer
- **Testing Equipment**: Timing validation hardware suite ($10-15k estimated)
- **Cloud Infrastructure**: CI/CD, monitoring, private npm registry
- **Software Licenses**: Development tools, monitoring services

### Budget Estimates
- **Team (6 months)**: $400-500k (depending on location/seniority)
- **Equipment**: $25-30k (hardware, testing, infrastructure)  
- **Services**: $5-10k (cloud, monitoring, tools)
- **Total Sprint 1-5**: $430-540k estimated

---

## Success Dependencies

### Technical Milestones
1. **Sprint 1**: ≤2ms timing precision demonstrated
2. **Sprint 2**: Complete experiment compiles and executes
3. **Sprint 3**: GUI provides intuitive experiment building
4. **Sprint 5**: Real-time collaboration functional
5. **Sprint 10**: 3 founding labs running production studies

### Strategic Milestones  
1. **Academic Paper**: Timing validation study submitted to peer review
2. **Founding Labs**: 5 research partnerships established
3. **DOI Integration**: Automatic citation system functional
4. **Network Effects**: First viral experiment sharing demonstrated
5. **Market Validation**: Clear demand from research community

---

## Risk Monitoring

### High-Risk Items Requiring Attention
- **Timing Precision Validation**: Single point of failure for entire value proposition
- **Academic Community Adoption**: Without researcher buy-in, technical excellence is meaningless
- **Team Formation**: Need senior developers with specific expertise
- **Browser API Stability**: Dependency on evolving web standards

### Early Warning Indicators
- Timing validation tests failing on any target platform
- Academic advisor feedback indicating skepticism
- Difficulty recruiting qualified development team
- Web API deprecation or restriction announcements

---

*Next tasks updated weekly. Priority items reviewed daily during active development.*

**Current Focus**: Complete sprint designs to enable full project planning and team formation.

# Progress Tracking - Psycho-Forge Development

**Project:** Psycho-Forge - The Scientific Operating System for Behavioral Research  
**Current Phase:** Pre-Development Planning  
**Last Updated:** July 2025

---

## Completed Items

### 2025-07-08 - Project Foundation Documents
- **Completed by**: Claude (AI Assistant)
- **Audit Status**: Pending - Pre-development phase
- **Items Completed**:
  - [x] Enhanced and restructured proposal.md with strategic insights
  - [x] Created CLAUDE.md with development rules and quality gates
  - [x] Designed sprint1.md - Runtime Core & Precision Timing Foundation
  - [x] Designed sprint2.md - Compiler & First Test

- **Notes**: 
  - Proposal enhanced with competitive analysis, technical architecture details, and go-to-market strategy
  - Development rules establish mandatory AI audit gates (Gemini Pro + O3) before sprint advancement
  - Sprint designs include comprehensive technical specifications and quality gates
- **Next Dependencies**: Establish development environment and team formation

### 2025-07-08 - Complete Sprint Designs 4-10
- **Completed by**: Claude (AI Assistant)
- **Audit Status**: Pending - Pre-development phase  
- **Items Completed**:
  - [x] Designed sprint4.md - Live Mirror-Canvas (CRDT sync, bidirectional editing)
  - [x] Designed sprint5.md - Simulation & Intelligence (AI assistance, time-travel debugging)
  - [x] Designed sprint6.md - Recruitment & Auth (URF implementation, OAuth2 PKCE)
  - [x] Designed sprint7.md - Ecosystem Foundations (Hub, DAL, paradigm library)
  - [x] Designed sprint8.md - Full-Cycle Workflow (ARE, MSG, export generation)
  - [x] Designed sprint9.md - Polish & Alpha Prep (performance optimization, documentation)
  - [x] Designed sprint10.md - Alpha Release (deployment, founding lab onboarding)
- **Notes**: 
  - All 10 sprints now fully designed with comprehensive technical specifications
  - Complete 10-sprint roadmap ready for development team
  - All quality gates and acceptance criteria defined
- **Next Dependencies**: Team formation and development environment setup

---

## Quality Metrics (Current Sprint: Pre-Development)

### Documentation Coverage
- **Strategic Planning**: 100% (Proposal complete with competitive analysis, technical architecture, financial model)
- **Development Process**: 100% (CLAUDE.md establishes complete development rules and quality gates)  
- **Sprint Planning**: 100% (All 10 sprints designed with comprehensive technical specifications)
- **Project Management**: 100% (progress.md and next.md established and maintained)

### Technical Foundation
- **Architecture Decisions**: Documented (TypeScript fluent API vs custom DSL, Y.js for CRDTs, etc.)
- **Performance Targets**: Defined (≤2ms timing, 144Hz refresh rate, <100MB memory)
- **Quality Standards**: Established (90% test coverage, WCAG 2.2 AA compliance, mandatory audits)
- **Security Framework**: Planned (CSP/SRI, OAuth2 PKCE, no data monetization policy)

### Strategic Enhancements Completed
- **Competitive Positioning**: Sharp differentiation vs jsPsych, PsychoPy, Inquisit, Gorilla
- **Network Effects Strategy**: DOI integration with DataCite/Zenodo for academic legitimacy
- **Technical Risk Mitigation**: Pivot from custom DSL to TypeScript fluent API reduces development risk
- **Go-to-Market Strategy**: Academic legitimacy first via peer-reviewed publication

---

## Sprint Progress Overview

| Sprint | Status | Key Deliverables | Completion |
|--------|--------|-----------------|------------|
| **0** | Planning | Foundation documents, development rules | 100% |
| **1** | Designed | Runtime core, precision timing, hierarchical scheduler | Design Complete |
| **2** | Designed | ForgeScript compiler, first working experiment | Design Complete |
| **3** | Designed | SvelteKit GUI, visual timeline, Monaco editor | Design Complete |
| **4** | Designed | Live Mirror-Canvas with CRDT sync | Design Complete |
| **5** | Designed | Simulation & AI assistance | Design Complete |
| **6** | Designed | Recruitment fabric & authentication | Design Complete |
| **7** | Designed | Ecosystem foundations | Design Complete |
| **8** | Designed | Full-cycle workflow | Design Complete |
| **9** | Designed | Polish & alpha preparation | Design Complete |
| **10** | Designed | Alpha release | Design Complete |

---

## Key Achievements

### Strategic Document Enhancement
✅ **Proposal Transformation**: Elevated from basic technical document to comprehensive strategic roadmap with:
- Sharp competitive differentiation strategy
- Academic legitimacy-first go-to-market approach  
- Technical architecture pivot to reduce risk (TypeScript fluent API)
- DOI integration strategy for viral network effects
- Clear headline messaging: "Ending the Reproducibility Crisis Through Precision Engineering"

### Development Framework Establishment  
✅ **Quality Gate System**: Created mandatory audit process requiring:
- Gemini Pro approval for architecture, code quality, performance
- O3 approval for technical implementation, algorithms, edge cases
- No sprint advancement without both approvals
- Comprehensive testing and documentation requirements

### Technical Foundation
✅ **Architecture Decisions**: Key technical choices documented:
- TypeScript fluent API instead of custom DSL (major risk reduction)
- Y.js for CRDT-based real-time collaboration
- Web Audio API + RequestAnimationFrame for ≤2ms timing precision
- PixiJS WebGL2 for high-performance stimulus rendering
- SvelteKit + Vite for modern development experience

---

## Lessons Learned

### Strategic Insights
- **Academic Trust is Paramount**: Peer-reviewed publication must come before marketing or adoption efforts
- **Technical Risk Reduction**: Leveraging existing ecosystems (TypeScript) vs building custom solutions
- **Network Effects Design**: DOI integration creates academic currency for viral sharing
- **Competitive Advantage**: Target architectural weaknesses, not feature comparison

### Development Process
- **Quality Gates Work**: Mandatory external audits prevent technical debt accumulation
- **Documentation First**: Comprehensive planning accelerates development and prevents scope creep
- **Accessibility Foundation**: WCAG compliance from day one, not retrofitted later
- **Performance Budgets**: Specific timing targets ensure precision requirements aren't compromised

---

## Current Blockers

### None (Pre-Development Phase)
- All planned pre-development tasks on track
- No technical blockers identified
- Development team formation pending

### Upcoming Risks
- **Development Team**: Need to establish core development team with TypeScript expertise
- **Hardware Validation**: Timing precision validation requires specialized testing equipment
- **Academic Partnerships**: Need founding labs for validation and early adoption

---

## Next Immediate Tasks

See [next.md](next.md) for detailed upcoming work queue.

**Priority 1**: ✅ Complete sprint designs 4-10 (COMPLETED)  
**Priority 2**: Establish development environment and team  
**Priority 3**: Begin Sprint 1 development with timing precision validation  

---

content.content:
1→# Sprint 1: Runtime Core & Precision Timing Foundation
     2→
     3→**Duration:** 2 weeks  
     4→**Sprint Goal:** Establish the foundational runtime kernel with verifiable precision timing capabilities  
     5→**Success Criteria:** Achieve ≤2ms stimulus onset timing on target hardware configuration
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Sprint Objectives
    10→
    11→1. **High-Precision Clock Implementation**: Build microsecond-accurate timing foundation using Web Audio API
    12→2. **Hierarchical Scheduler**: Implement the core scheduling system for experiment execution
    13→3. **Timing Validation Framework**: Create comprehensive timing measurement and validation tools
    14→4. **Core Data Structures**: Define fundamental experiment representation formats
    15→5. **Performance Baseline**: Establish timing benchmarks across target platforms
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## Sprint Backlog
    20→
    21→### Epic: High-Precision Timing System
    22→**Story Points: 8**
    23→
    24→#### KER-001: High-Precision Clock Implementation
    25→**Points: 5 | Priority: Critical**
    26→
    27→**Description:** Implement the core timing system that will serve as the foundation for all experiment timing guarantees.
    28→
    29→**Technical Requirements:**
    30→- Integrate Web Audio API hardware clock for microsecond precision
    31→- Implement RequestAnimationFrame synchronization for display timing
    32→- Create Performance.now() wrapper for high-resolution timestamps
    33→- Build timing drift detection and correction mechanisms
    34→
    35→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    36→- [ ] Web Audio context provides stable, monotonic timestamps
    37→- [ ] RAF callbacks synchronized with display refresh rate
    38→- [ ] Performance.now() measurements accurate to 5μs resolution
    39→- [ ] Timing drift detection warns when precision degrades
    40→- [ ] Cross-browser compatibility validated (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
    41→
    42→**Technical Implementation:**
    43→```typescript
    44→// @psyforge/timing
    45→export class PrecisionClock {
    46→  private audioContext: AudioContext;
    47→  private rafCallbacks: Set<(timestamp: number) => void>;
    48→  private driftDetector: TimingDriftDetector;
    49→  
    50→  constructor() {
    51→    this.audioContext = new AudioContext();
    52→    this.rafCallbacks = new Set();
    53→    this.driftDetector = new TimingDriftDetector();
    54→  }
    55→  
    56→  // Get hardware-precision timestamp
    57→  now(): number;
    58→  
    59→  // Schedule callback at specific time
    60→  schedule(callback: () => void, targetTime: number): void;
    61→  
    62→  // Validate timing environment
    63→  validateEnvironment(): TimingCapabilities;
    64→}
    65→```
    66→
    67→---
    68→
    69→#### KER-002: Hierarchical Scheduler Implementation  
    70→**Points: 3 | Priority: High**
    71→
    72→**Description:** Build the core experiment execution scheduler that manages timelines, trials, and stimulus presentation.
    73→
    74→**Technical Requirements:**
    75→- Implement priority-based task scheduling
    76→- Support nested timeline execution with inheritance
    77→- Handle concurrent task management
    78→- Provide frame-accurate stimulus timing
    79→
    80→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    81→- [ ] Schedule tasks with millisecond precision
    82→- [ ] Support timeline hierarchy (experiment → timeline → trial → stimulus)
    83→- [ ] Handle stimulus onset/offset timing accurately
    84→- [ ] Manage concurrent executions without timing conflicts
    85→- [ ] Provide debugging/profiling hooks for timing analysis
    86→
    87→**Technical Implementation:**
    88→```typescript
    89→// @psyforge/scheduler
    90→export class HierarchicalScheduler {
    91→  private clock: PrecisionClock;
    92→  private taskQueue: PriorityQueue<ScheduledTask>;
    93→  private activeTimelines: Map<string, Timeline>;
    94→  
    95→  // Schedule experiment execution
    96→  scheduleExperiment(experiment: Experiment): Promise<void>;
    97→  
    98→  // Execute timeline with precise timing
    99→  executeTimeline(timeline: Timeline): Promise<void>;
   100→  
   101→  // Handle stimulus presentation
   102→  presentStimulus(stimulus: Stimulus, timing: TimingSpec): Promise<void>;
   103→}
   104→```
   105→
   106→---
   107→
   108→### Epic: Timing Validation & Benchmarking
   109→**Story Points: 8**
   110→
   111→#### TST-001: Timing Benchmark Harness v1

# Sprint 1: Runtime Core & Precision Timing Foundation

**Duration:** 2 weeks  
**Sprint Goal:** Establish the foundational runtime kernel with verifiable precision timing capabilities  
**Success Criteria:** Achieve ≤2ms stimulus onset timing on target hardware configuration

---

## Sprint Objectives

1. **High-Precision Clock Implementation**: Build microsecond-accurate timing foundation using Web Audio API
2. **Hierarchical Scheduler**: Implement the core scheduling system for experiment execution
3. **Timing Validation Framework**: Create comprehensive timing measurement and validation tools
4. **Core Data Structures**: Define fundamental experiment representation formats
5. **Performance Baseline**: Establish timing benchmarks across target platforms

---

## Sprint Backlog

### Epic: High-Precision Timing System
**Story Points: 8**

#### KER-001: High-Precision Clock Implementation
**Points: 5 | Priority: Critical**

**Description:** Implement the core timing system that will serve as the foundation for all experiment timing guarantees.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Integrate Web Audio API hardware clock for microsecond precision
- Implement RequestAnimationFrame synchronization for display timing
- Create Performance.now() wrapper for high-resolution timestamps
- Build timing drift detection and correction mechanisms

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Web Audio context provides stable, monotonic timestamps
- [ ] RAF callbacks synchronized with display refresh rate
- [ ] Performance.now() measurements accurate to 5μs resolution
- [ ] Timing drift detection warns when precision degrades
- [ ] Cross-browser compatibility validated (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)

**Technical Implementation:**
```typescript
// @psyforge/timing
export class PrecisionClock {
  private audioContext: AudioContext;
  private rafCallbacks: Set<(timestamp: number) => void>;
  private driftDetector: TimingDriftDetector;
  
  constructor() {
    this.audioContext = new AudioContext();
    this.rafCallbacks = new Set();
    this.driftDetector = new TimingDriftDetector();
  }
  
  // Get hardware-precision timestamp
  now(): number;
  
  // Schedule callback at specific time
  schedule(callback: () => void, targetTime: number): void;
  
  // Validate timing environment
  validateEnvironment(): TimingCapabilities;
}
```

---

#### KER-002: Hierarchical Scheduler Implementation  
**Points: 3 | Priority: High**

**Description:** Build the core experiment execution scheduler that manages timelines, trials, and stimulus presentation.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Implement priority-based task scheduling
- Support nested timeline execution with inheritance
- Handle concurrent task management
- Provide frame-accurate stimulus timing

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Schedule tasks with millisecond precision
- [ ] Support timeline hierarchy (experiment → timeline → trial → stimulus)
- [ ] Handle stimulus onset/offset timing accurately
- [ ] Manage concurrent executions without timing conflicts
- [ ] Provide debugging/profiling hooks for timing analysis

**Technical Implementation:**
```typescript
// @psyforge/scheduler
export class HierarchicalScheduler {
  private clock: PrecisionClock;
  private taskQueue: PriorityQueue<ScheduledTask>;
  private activeTimelines: Map<string, Timeline>;
  
  // Schedule experiment execution
  scheduleExperiment(experiment: Experiment): Promise<void>;
  
  // Execute timeline with precise timing
  executeTimeline(timeline: Timeline): Promise<void>;
  
  // Handle stimulus presentation
  presentStimulus(stimulus: Stimulus, timing: TimingSpec): Promise<void>;
}
```

---

### Epic: Timing Validation & Benchmarking
**Story Points: 8**

#### TST-001: Timing Benchmark Harness v1
**Points: 5 | Priority: High**

**Description:** Create comprehensive timing validation tools to verify and document precision capabilities.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Build automated timing benchmark suite
- Implement photodiode/oscilloscope integration for external validation
- Create cross-platform timing capability assessment
- Generate precision validation reports

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Automated benchmark measures stimulus onset accuracy
- [ ] External hardware validation (photodiode) confirms timing
- [ ] Cross-browser/OS timing capability matrix generated
- [ ] Performance regression detection implemented
- [ ] Timing validation reports exportable for publication

**Hardware Requirements:**
- High-speed camera (240fps+) for visual validation
- Photodiode + oscilloscope for electrical validation  
- Multiple test systems (Windows/Mac/Linux + different browsers)

---

#### KER-005: Cross-OS Timer Drift Detection
**Points: 3 | Priority: Medium**

**Description:** Implement detection and mitigation for OS-level timer throttling and drift.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Detect browser tab visibility throttling
- Identify OS power management interference
- Warn users when timing environment is compromised
- Provide automatic environment optimization suggestions

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Detect visibility-based timer throttling
- [ ] Identify OS power management impacts
- [ ] Display real-time timing health indicators
- [ ] Provide actionable optimization recommendations
- [ ] Log timing degradation events for analysis

---

### Epic: Core Data Structures & API Design
**Story Points: 7**

#### DSL-001: Define Initial AST Schema (Zod)
**Points: 4 | Priority: Critical**

**Description:** Define the canonical Abstract Syntax Tree schema that represents experiments.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Create Zod schemas for all experiment components
- Define TypeScript types from schemas
- Implement runtime validation
- Support schema versioning and migration

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Complete Zod schema for Experiment, Timeline, Trial, Stimulus
- [ ] TypeScript types auto-generated from schemas
- [ ] Runtime validation prevents invalid experiment structures
- [ ] Schema versioning supports backward compatibility
- [ ] JSON Schema generation for interoperability

**Schema Structure:**
```typescript
// Core experiment components
const ExperimentSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  name: z.string(),
  version: z.string(),
  timelines: z.array(TimelineSchema),
  metadata: z.record(z.any()).optional()
});

const TimelineSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  trials: z.array(TrialSchema),
  randomization: RandomizationSchema.optional(),
  repetitions: z.number().default(1)
});

const TrialSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  stimuli: z.array(StimulusSchema),
  responses: z.array(ResponseSchema),
  timing: TimingSchema
});
```

---

#### DSL-002: Implement FZB Serialization
**Points: 3 | Priority: High**

**Description:** Create the Forge Binary AST (FZB) serialization format for canonical experiment representation.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Protocol Buffers schema for binary serialization
- Version-pinned experiment representation
- Efficient serialization/deserialization
- Cross-platform compatibility

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Protocol Buffers schema matches Zod schema structure
- [ ] Binary serialization reduces experiment size by >70%
- [ ] Deserialization preserves exact experiment semantics
- [ ] Version information embedded in binary format
- [ ] Cross-language compatibility (TypeScript, Python, R)

---

### Epic: Infrastructure & Development Setup
**Story Points: 5**

#### INF-001: Stand up Monorepo & CI/CD
**Points: 3 | Priority: Critical**

**Description:** Establish the development infrastructure for the multi-package Psycho-Forge ecosystem.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Nx monorepo with TypeScript packages
- Automated testing and linting in CI
- Package publishing to private npm registry
- Code quality gates and PR validation

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Nx workspace configured with core packages
- [ ] GitHub Actions CI runs tests on all PRs
- [ ] ESLint + Prettier enforced across codebase
- [ ] Private npm registry configured for package distribution
- [ ] PR merge requires passing tests + code review

**Package Structure:**
```
packages/
├── core/           # Core experiment types and utilities
├── timing/         # High-precision timing implementation
├── scheduler/      # Hierarchical task scheduler
├── runtime/        # Experiment execution engine
├── serialization/  # FZB binary format handling
└── validation/     # Schema validation and testing
```

---

#### ARC-001: Spike: Kernel-to-Renderer PoC
**Points: 2 | Priority: Medium**

**Description:** Proof-of-concept for integrating timing kernel with PixiJS rendering pipeline.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Basic PixiJS application with precise timing
- Demonstrate stimulus presentation with measured accuracy
- Validate WebGL2 performance characteristics
- Establish rendering pipeline architecture

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] PixiJS renders stimuli with frame-accurate timing
- [ ] WebGL2 pipeline bypasses DOM for critical path
- [ ] Stimulus presentation measured under 2ms variability
- [ ] Rendering architecture supports complex stimulus types
- [ ] Performance profiling shows rendering bottlenecks

---

## Definition of Done

**For each ticket:**
- [ ] Code implemented and unit tested (>90% coverage)
- [ ] Integration tests validate cross-component functionality
- [ ] Performance benchmarks meet sprint targets
- [ ] Documentation updated (API docs + implementation notes)
- [ ] Code review completed by another team member
- [ ] **Gemini Pro code audit approval received**
- [ ] **O3 (via zen mcp) code audit approval received**

**For sprint completion:**
- [ ] All tickets meet DoD criteria
- [ ] ≤2ms timing demonstrated on target hardware
- [ ] Timing validation report generated and reviewed
- [ ] Technical debt items documented for future sprints
- [ ] Sprint retrospective completed with lessons learned

---

## Quality Gates & Audit Requirements

### Pre-Sprint Advancement Checklist

**Code Quality Audit (Gemini Pro):**
- [ ] Architecture review confirms scalable design patterns
- [ ] Code quality meets enterprise standards
- [ ] Performance characteristics validated
- [ ] Security considerations addressed
- [ ] Documentation completeness verified

**Technical Implementation Audit (O3 via zen mcp):**
- [ ] Algorithm correctness validated
- [ ] Edge case handling comprehensive
- [ ] Error conditions properly managed
- [ ] Integration points well-defined
- [ ] Testing strategy covers critical paths

**Acceptance Testing:**
- [ ] All unit tests pass (>90% coverage)
- [ ] Integration tests validate end-to-end workflows
- [ ] Performance benchmarks meet timing requirements
- [ ] Cross-platform validation completed
- [ ] Documentation reviewed and approved

---

## Risk Mitigation

**Technical Risks:**
- **Web Audio API limitations**: Fallback to Performance.now() with degraded precision warnings
- **Cross-browser timing variations**: Comprehensive testing matrix with environment-specific optimizations
- **Performance bottlenecks**: Early profiling and benchmark-driven optimization

**Delivery Risks:**
- **Complex timing requirements**: Focus on single-platform validation first, expand gradually
- **Integration complexity**: Modular architecture allows independent development and testing

---

## Sprint Success Metrics

**Primary Metrics:**
- **Timing Precision**: ≤2ms stimulus onset jitter on target hardware
- **Performance**: Sustain 144Hz refresh rate with complex stimuli
- **Reliability**: Zero timing failures in 1000-trial test sequences

**Secondary Metrics:**
- **Code Quality**: 90%+ test coverage, zero critical issues in audits
- **Documentation**: Complete API documentation for all public interfaces
- **Team Velocity**: Sprint completed within 2-week timeframe

---

*Next: [Sprint 2: Compiler & First Test](sprint2.md)*

1→# Sprint 2: Compiler & First Test
     2→
     3→**Duration:** 2 weeks  
     4→**Sprint Goal:** Build the ForgeScript TypeScript fluent API compiler and create the first working experiment  
     5→**Success Criteria:** Successfully compile and execute a basic Stroop task with validated timing
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Sprint Objectives
    10→
    11→1. **ForgeScript Fluent API**: Implement the TypeScript-based experiment definition API
    12→2. **Incremental Compiler**: Build the compilation pipeline from API calls to executable experiments
    13→3. **First Working Experiment**: Create and validate a complete Stroop task implementation
    14→4. **Data Schema Lock**: Establish BIDS-compatible data export format
    15→5. **Timing Validation**: Demonstrate <2ms precision on compiled experiments
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## Sprint Backlog
    20→
    21→### Epic: ForgeScript Compiler Implementation
    22→**Story Points: 10**
    23→
    24→#### DSL-003: Build TypeScript Fluent API & Compiler
    25→**Points: 8 | Priority: Critical**
    26→
    27→**Description:** Implement the core ForgeScript fluent API that allows declarative experiment definition using TypeScript.
    28→
    29→**Technical Requirements:**
    30→- Fluent API classes for Experiment, Timeline, Trial, Stimulus, Response
    31→- Compile-time and runtime validation through Zod schemas
    32→- Type-safe builder pattern with IntelliSense support
    33→- Integration with formal verification checks
    34→
    35→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    36→- [ ] Complete fluent API covers all experiment components
    37→- [ ] TypeScript IntelliSense provides comprehensive autocompletion
    38→- [ ] Compile-time validation prevents invalid experiment structures
    39→- [ ] Runtime validation catches configuration errors
    40→- [ ] API compiles to FZB binary format
    41→- [ ] Support for power analysis integration (`requirePower()`)
    42→
    43→**API Implementation:**
    44→```typescript
    45→// @psyforge/api
    46→export class Experiment {
    47→  constructor(config: ExperimentConfig);
    48→  addTimeline(timeline: Timeline): this;
    49→  setRecruitment(config: RecruitmentConfig): this;
    50→  validate(): ValidationResult;
    51→  requirePower(config: PowerAnalysisConfig): this;
    52→  compile(): CompiledExperiment;
    53→}
    54→
    55→export class Timeline {
    56→  constructor(config: TimelineConfig);
    57→  addTrial(trial: Trial): this;
    58→  setRandomization(config: RandomizationConfig): this;
    59→  repeat(count: number): this;
    60→}
    61→
    62→export class Trial {
    63→  constructor(config?: TrialConfig);
    64→  addStimulus(stimulus: Stimulus): this;
    65→  addResponse(response: Response): this;
    66→  setTiming(timing: TimingConfig): this;
    67→}
    68→
    69→export namespace Stimulus {
    70→  export class Text {
    71→    constructor(config: TextStimulusConfig);
    72→  }
    73→  
    74→  export class Image {
    75→    constructor(config: ImageStimulusConfig);
    76→  }
    77→  
    78→  export class Audio {
    79→    constructor(config: AudioStimulusConfig);
    80→  }
    81→}
    82→
    83→export namespace Response {
    84→  export class KeyPress {
    85→    constructor(config: KeyPressConfig);
    86→  }
    87→  
    88→  export class MouseClick {
    89→    constructor(config: MouseClickConfig);
    90→  }
    91→}
    92→```
    93→
    94→**Example Usage:**
    95→```typescript
    96→const stroopTask = new Experiment({ name: 'Stroop Task' })
    97→  .addTimeline(
    98→    new Timeline({ id: 'practice' })
    99→      .addTrial(
   100→        new Trial()
   101→          .addStimulus(new Stimulus.Text({
   102→            content: 'RED',
   103→            color: 'blue',
   104→            fontSize: 48,
   105→            timing: { onset: 500, duration: 1000 }
   106→          }))
   107→          .addResponse(new Response.KeyPress({
   108→            keys: ['r', 'g', 'b', 'y'],
   109→            correctKey: 'r',
   110→            timeout: 2000
   111→          }))
   112→      )
   113→      .repeat(10)
   114→  )
   115→  .requirePower({ effect: 0.5, alpha: 0.05, target: 0.8 })
   116→  .validate()
   117→  .compile();
   118→```
   119→
   120→---
   121→
   122→#### DSL-003a: Spike: TypeScript AST Analysis vs Babel
   123→**Points: 2 | Priority: Medium**
   124→
   125→**Description:** Evaluate TypeScript compiler API vs Babel for advanced static analysis capabilities.
   126→
   127→**Technical Requirements:**
   128→- Compare ts-morph vs @babel/parser for AST manipulation
   129→- Evaluate performance characteristics for incremental compilation
   130→- Assess macro expansion capabilities
   131→- Determine tooling support and ecosystem integration
   132→
   133→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   134→- [ ] Performance comparison completed (parse time, memory usage)
   135→- [ ] AST manipulation capabilities evaluated
   136→- [ ] Incremental compilation support assessed
   137→- [ ] Recommendation documented with rationale
   138→- [ ] Proof-of-concept implemented with chosen approach
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→### Epic: First Working Experiment
   143→**Story Points: 6**
   144→
   145→#### KER-003: Implement Core Stimulus Types
   146→**Points: 4 | Priority: High**
   147→
   148→**Description:** Build the fundamental stimulus presentation system supporting text, images, and basic geometric shapes.
   149→
   150→**Technical Requirements:**
   151→- PixiJS-based stimulus rendering with WebGL2 acceleration
   152→- Precise timing control for stimulus onset/offset
   153→- Support for text, image, and geometric stimuli
   154→- Efficient asset preloading and memory management
   155→
   156→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   157→- [ ] Text stimuli render with configurable fonts, colors, sizes
   158→- [ ] Image stimuli support common formats (PNG, JPG, SVG)
   159→- [ ] Geometric shapes (rectangles, circles, polygons) available
   160→- [ ] Stimulus timing accurate to display refresh rate
   161→- [ ] Asset preloading prevents timing delays
   162→- [ ] Memory usage optimized for large stimulus sets
   163→
   164→**Stimulus Types Implementation:**
   165→```typescript
   166→// Text stimulus with precise positioning
   167→const textStimulus = new Stimulus.Text({
   168→  content: 'Hello World',
   169→  fontSize: 24,
   170→  fontFamily: 'Arial',
   171→  color: '#FF0000',
   172→  position: { x: 0, y: 0 }, // Screen center
   173→  timing: {
   174→    onset: 1000,     // 1 second after trial start
   175→    duration: 2000   // Display for 2 seconds
   176→  }
   177→});
   178→
   179→// Image stimulus with scaling
   180→const imageStimulus = new Stimulus.Image({
